subsections.text: In this chapter:
“Department” means the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles.
“Disability” means a condition in which a person has:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: mobility problems that substantially impair the person’s ability to ambulate;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: visual acuity of 20/200 or less in the better eye with correcting lenses; or

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: visual acuity of more than 20/200 but with a limited field of vision in which the widest diameter of the visual field subtends an angle of 20 degrees or less.
“Disabled parking placard” means a placard issued under Section 681.002 (Disabled Parking Placard) .
“International symbol of access” means the symbol adopted by Rehabilitation International in 1969 at its Eleventh World Congress on Rehabilitation of the Disabled.
“Mobility problem that substantially impairs a person’s ability to ambulate” means that the person:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: cannot walk 200 feet without stopping to rest;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: cannot walk without the use of or assistance from an assistance device, including a brace, a cane, a crutch, another person, or a prosthetic device;

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: cannot ambulate without a wheelchair or similar device;

subsections.label: D
subsections.text: is restricted by lung disease to the extent that the person’s forced respiratory expiratory volume for one second, measured by spirometry, is less than one liter, or the arterial oxygen tension is less than 60 millimeters of mercury on room air at rest;

subsections.label: E
subsections.text: uses portable oxygen;

subsections.label: F
subsections.text: has a cardiac condition to the extent that the person’s functional limitations are classified in severity as Class III or Class IV according to standards set by the American Heart Association;

subsections.label: G
subsections.text: is severely limited in the ability to walk because of an arthritic, neurological, or orthopedic condition;

subsections.label: H
subsections.text: has a disorder of the foot that, in the opinion of a person licensed to practice podiatry in this state or in a state adjacent to this state, limits or impairs the person’s ability to walk; or

subsections.label: I
subsections.text: has another debilitating condition that, in the opinion of a physician licensed to practice medicine in this state or a state adjacent to this state, or authorized by applicable law to practice medicine in a hospital or other health facility of the Veterans Administration, limits or impairs the person’s ability to walk.
“Podiatry” has the meaning assigned by Section 202.001 (Definitions) , Occupations Code.
“Stand” or “standing” means to halt an occupied or unoccupied vehicle, other than temporarily while receiving or discharging passengers.

history.raw: Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 933 (H.B.3097), Sec. 2P.01, eff. September 1, 2009.
